profile.name: core-ops
profile.intent: Provide the shared operational substrate for memory, git closeout, docs, sidecars, validation, workspaces, and safe project maintenance across project types.

routing.handoff_policy: Enter through project-ops-router, then hand off to mature owner skills such as safe-git-ops, research-project-memory, update-docs, sidecar-task-runner, or work-timeline-planner. Remote-runtime and scheduler-probe work belongs to the sibling `automation` profile and should not be duplicated here.

install_policy.notes: Core operations should be available across many project types, but full ML research lifecycle skills should not be pulled in by default.

workflow_contract.lane_selection_rule: Use combined for most durable ops because mutation should follow state inspection; use action for bounded known writes, evidence for audits, and no-contract only for transient read-only answers.

required_preflight: Detect repository scope, worktree status, and dirty state before writes.
Read project conventions for wrappers and forbidden command shapes.
Classify the closeout path before validation, commit, push, or release actions.
forbidden_actions: Do not run destructive Git operations without explicit approval.
Do not push with raw git push when a project-push wrapper is required.
Do not publish private memory, credentials, raw logs, or local machine facts into public docs.
action_lane.approval_boundary: Escalate before destructive recovery, force push, credential or environment changes, public release, branch deletion, or broad unrelated cleanup.

evidence_lane.confidence_policy: Treat live command output as execution truth and memory as coordination context; write only durable summaries back to memory.
evidence_lane.citation_policy: Cite repo-local paths and command names; avoid copying raw logs or private session transcripts into public artifacts.

combined_lane.handoff_artifact: preflight summary, validation result, commit hash, status artifact, or memory update
combined_lane.staleness_trigger: Dirty state changes, branch divergence, wrapper policy changes, failing validators, or new sidecar/remote output.

no_contract_lane.allowed_when: The task is read-only, transient, and does not change files, memory, Git state, remotes, or public surfaces.
no_contract_lane.escalation_trigger: The task requires mutation, credentialed access, durable memory, public output, or a remote/network operation.

acceptance_checks: Kernel example references real core-ops profile skills and router.
Action and combined lanes name mutation, wrapper, approval, and rollback boundaries.
Memory writeback distinguishes durable summaries from private or raw operational logs.

privacy_boundary.publication_audit_required_when: Use memory-publication-auditor before turning private operational lessons, logs, or local memories into public core-ops rules.

selection_contexts: Select core-ops directly when it is installed as the only operational profile entrypoint.
Select core-ops when a runtime or prompt explicitly asks for profile-first routing.
Do not require core-ops to beat mature leaf or router skills when a full shared skill root is also visible.
selection_semantics.delegation_policy: In a full shared skill root, core-ops may act as a substrate and allow project-ops-router, safe-git-ops, update-docs, research-project-memory, or other mature owner skills to be selected directly.
selection_semantics.installable_manifest_gate: Installable manifests must preserve profile-first or isolated-profile activation semantics; prompt visibility alone is not sufficient evidence that core-ops should override mature leaf skills.

promotion_gates: Core operations can be installed without pulling ML-specific lifecycle skills.
Project memory, Git closeout, docs, sidecar, validation, and remote wrappers remain coherent after extraction.
Runtime adapters can be generated without duplicating source logic in Codex or Claude Code metadata.
rejection_checks: Do not extract if core-ops still depends on ML-specific claims or experiment semantics.
Do not make project-private paths or credentials part of public kernel examples.
Do not split before profile registry, validators, and docs agree on install boundaries.
